taskName: Release blocker fix - stabilize same-WiFi LAN reachability
goal: Fix the FE-V103 release blocker where FileEasy becomes unreachable from another same-WiFi device after initial successful access, while the APK still reports the service as running and Android shows port 3000 listening.
nonGoals: Do not fix the admin wrong-landing blocker in this task unless it is proven to be the same root cause as LAN reachability.
Do not fix the authenticated /api/v1/files 404 blocker in this task unless it is proven to be the same root cause as LAN reachability.
Do not add hotspot automation, discovery, public access, or new product features.
Do not re-enable player, monitor dashboard, or Xplay-only UI in FileEasy mode.
Do not modify PRD or design documents.

acceptanceChecks.text: After FileEasy is launched and the APK homepage shows a Wi-Fi upload URL, another device on the same Wi-Fi can repeatedly access http://<device-ip>:3000/api/v1/ping and http://<device-ip>:3000/ without intermittent timeout, connection refused, or empty response failures.

The FileEasy foreground service remains reachable while the service is shown as running, including after opening/closing the APK admin WebView and after the app moves between foreground and background.
The fix does not reintroduce player UI, monitor dashboard entry, autodiscovery, public-network behavior, or legacy upload APIs into the FileEasy product boundary.
The APK still binds the local HTTP server to a LAN-reachable address on port 3000 and still displays a usable upload URL/QR for the currently selected LAN interface.

verificationCommands: for i in {1..10}; do curl -sS -m 5 http://<device-ip>:3000/api/v1/ping; echo; sleep 1; done
handoffQuestions: Was the root cause lifecycle, address selection, Android foreground-service behavior, server binding, or something else?
If admin wrong-landing or authenticated /api/v1/files 404 remains after this fix, list them as separate blockers rather than expanding this task.
